description.value:
Used as a replacement for skills rolled with base COOL, EMP, or WILL

Used for rolling in checks which involve force of will, charisma, general intelligence, or intution. Note that if the check is specialized and the mook is not, the DV should be raised 1-3 tiers.

Used as a replacement for fighting or ranged weapon skills, excluding evasion. Note that this uses REF as a base for reasons involving initiative - set REF of the mook first, then consider that number when considering the Martial skill.
Used as a replacement for skills which rely on base Body or Dex, which are not covered by the Martial skill, and for Evasion.
Used for skills which rely on base INT or TECH.
A concealable, medium-sized pistol. +1 to Attack.
A concealable, medium-sized pistol. +1 to Attack.
Armor Item for Subdermal Armor Cyberware.
Turns weapon into a "Smartgun". Refer to all rules specified on pg. 344 before using.
Turns weapon into a "Smartgun". Refer to all rules specified on pg. 344 before using.
An artificial eye. Choose Kiroshi Optics!
Access to nervous system.
3d6 damage unless Resist Torture/Drugs. Armor does not apply.
Provides a +1 to aimed shots.
Must be paired. Provides immunity to dazzling effects (like flashbangs).
+2 Grooming with Chemskin. Color changing hair.
Single socket for installation and removal of a piece of chipware.
Chipware. Can detect motion up to 20m/yd away through surfaces.
A base interface for audio enhancements.
Provides +2 to perception checks that use hearing.
Provides +2 to Human Perception and Interrogation checks. Can activate a special lie-detecting mode.
Built in agent. Audio only but can be linked with other cybergear.
Tiny screen in your eye. Provides picture-in-picture for those boring moments.
Autotune synthesizer. Provides +2 to Acting and Play Instrument checks.
A sight midification for any ranged weapon. Provides +1 to hit against targets within 12m.
A sight midification for any ranged weapon. Provides +1 to hit against targets within 12m.

Req Targeting Scope Cyberware, otherwise won't fire. If miss single attack by 4 or less, roll second attack at same DV 1d10+10 (not normal stat). Can add luck. Target can dodge the second roll as normal if able to do so.
A cybernetical replacement arm. Comes with a free hand.
Plating on your hand which allows you to utilise Smartguns. Requires a neural link.
A concealable Melee Weapon. Can't hold anything with this hand while activated.
Plating on your hand which allows you to utilise Smartguns. Requires a neural link.

information.notes:
Mook Tier - Tier 4X/Isolated Elite Threat - Iron Sights members are specialized agents, who tend to work alone or in pairs.

Gang-Wide Ability - Concealed Identity: When placing one or more Iron Sights agents in a scene, they may disguise themselves flawlessly as any other type of mook, up to and including the acquisition of additional gear which the mook would normally be carrying. If they do so, their name changes to the other mook, they gain that mook's abilities in addition to their own, and are indistinguishable from a normal enemy with extra gear and abilities unless another character succeeds a DV21 Deduction or Conceal/Reveal Object check, indicating their true allegiances.

Ability - Discretionary Toxins/Social Engineering: When entering a scene, the fixer chooses a target. When that target is subject to the biotoxin effect, they must succeed a DV21 BODY resist drugs/torture instead of any other effect. If ingested by anyone, the afflicted individual rolls once a minute until they pass the check, are given an antidote by a medtech, or die. Additionally, as an action a Fixer can turn an otherwise-neutral scene hostile towards one or more player characters. Mooks will behave normally in these situations (civillians will run or not attempt to fight, etc.)

The Iron Sights fell on hard times after the war. I'm surprised they're still around. Nowadays they've rebranded as assassins and bodyguards for hire, like a discount Arasaka. They don't come cheap, but they're professional elites dedicated to the work. They specialize in infiltration, and discretion. Rumor has it that the Afterlife funds them, but nobody's talking.
